text: Tracking Cyclosporiasis: 30 more in Wake diagnosed with diarrhea-causing bug - WRAL
fullText: More than 30 people got a Cyclosporiasis diagnosis on Thursday in Wake County, pushing the total number of people dealing with the diarrhea-causing bug to 270.

text: FBI will no longer investigate ICE confrontations, The New York Times reports - CNBC
fullText: The Department of Justice and the Department of Homeland Security have both denied that any change in oversight has taken place.

text: World Cup Daily live: Final countdown as Spain, Argentina get ready to take New York - ESPN
fullText: Enjoy all the features, news, video analysis and color from around the World Cup as Spain and Lamine Yamal take on Argentina and Lionel Messi in the final.

text: Troy Jackson has effectively secured the Maine Senate Democratic nomination - Politico
fullText: Jackson’s preferred delegates have won a majority of the seats at next week’s nominating convention to replace Graham Platner.

text: Live updates: Iran war news; US troop deaths raise fears of a wider war as Iran hit by new strikes - CNN
fullText: An American service member was killed in Iraq on Saturday, the US military said a day after announcing two troops died in an Iranian attack in Jordan. Follow live updates.

text: ‘The Odyssey’ Becomes Christopher Nolan’s Biggest Global Debut of All Time With $264 Million - Variety
fullText: "The Odyssey" powered to $264 million globally, ranking as Christopher Nolan's biggest worldwide box office debut.

text: Stomach bugs surging as doctors say certain travel essentials could save your trip - Fox News
fullText: With travelers' diarrhea the most common illness during summer travel, the CDC says packing oral rehydration salts and loperamide can help manage symptoms.

text: The Open Championship: Ryan Fox drains the putt of his life to claim the Claret Jug - Yahoo Sports

text: Irish Open: Rory McIlroy says Bryson DeChambeau at Doonbeg would be great - BBC
fullText: Rory McIlroy says that having Bryson DeChambeau playing in the Irish Open in September "would be great for the tournament".

text: Reports: Thunder trade Lu Dort to Hawks in 3-team deal - NBA.com
fullText: Atlanta reportedly adds Lu Dort and Ryan Nembhard, Dallas lands Zaccharie Risacher and OKC gets draft picks.

text: 'Real Housewives Of Orange County' Star Jeana Keough Diagnosed With Tongue Cancer At 70 - Deadline
fullText: Jeana Keough, one of the OG stars of The Real Housewives of Orange County, has been diagnosed with tongue cancer at the age of 70, her daughter confirmed.

text: Tropical depression forms in Gulf, National Hurricane Center says - USA Today
fullText: The National Hurricane Center said a tropical depression formed as thunderstorms and rain increase along the Gulf Coast.

text: Second person dies of Legionnaires’ disease as New York City cases decline sharply - NBC News
fullText: A second person has died of Legionnaires’ disease in connection with an outbreak on New York City’s Upper East Side that has sickened 72 people and prompted cleanup efforts at the world-famous Metropolitan Museum of Art and Guggenheim Museum.

text: US and Iran trade strikes again as Washington avenges American deaths - politico.eu
fullText: Tehran hit Gulf states hosting American troops, while Washington said its bombing would “swiftly punish” Iran for killing two U.S. soldiers.

text: What to know about the Tate brothers, social media influencers facing rape and trafficking charges - AP News

text: Who is Andy Burnham, the U.K.'s new prime minister? - NPR
fullText: On Monday, former Manchester Mayor Andy Burnham succeeds Keir Starmer as the United Kingdom's prime minister. Burnham's politics have been shaped by England's north-south class divide.
